us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies at 71 after sudden illness
bullets: Graham’s office said the South Carolina Republican died Saturday evening after a brief, sudden illness.
He was a prominent Trump ally and a leading Senate voice on foreign policy, including Ukraine, Israel and Iran.
His death creates a vacancy in South Carolina’s U.S. Senate delegation.

articles.title: New heat wave puts 44 million Americans under warnings
bullets: Heat warnings covered about 44 million people as temperatures in parts of the Rockies and northern Plains were forecast near 43°C.
Forecasters warned of possible records in areas including Salt Lake City and northern states.
The heat also complicated wildfire response in parts of Colorado and Utah.

articles.title: Missouri flash floods trap hundreds after torrential storms
bullets: Storms dropped 6 to 12 inches of rain in southeastern Missouri, swelling the Black River and nearby streams.
National Guard helicopters helped evacuate about 200 people from Camp Taum Sauk, roughly half of them children.
Gov. Mike Kehoe declared a state of emergency for flood-hit areas.

articles.title: US extends work permits for some TPS immigrants hours before deadline
bullets: The Trump administration extended work authorization for hundreds of thousands of immigrants with Temporary Protected Status.
The move came shortly before permits were set to expire for Haitians and people from several other countries.
USCIS guidance set a July 24 date for Haitians with TPS, with other designations also facing near-term deadlines.

articles.title: Witnesses dispute ICE account of fatal Houston shooting
bullets: A lawyer said three detained witnesses challenged ICE’s account of the shooting death of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo in Houston.
ICE said Salgado rammed a vehicle and tried to run over an officer; the lawyer said witnesses described a different sequence.
The shooting prompted protests and calls for an independent investigation.

global.label: Global
global.subtitle: Top worldwide stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Joint statement rejects China’s South China Sea maritime claims
bullets: Japan, the Philippines, the United States and 11 other countries said China’s expansive claims have no legal basis.
The statement marked the 10th anniversary of a 2016 international tribunal ruling favoring the Philippines.
Beijing has continued to reject the ruling as maritime tensions persist.

articles.title: Typhoon Bavi batters eastern China; Taiwan reports 134 injured
bullets: Emergency crews in eastern China cleared fallen trees and flooded streets after Typhoon Bavi moved inland.
Authorities evacuated nearly 2 million people before the storm’s arrival, mostly in Zhejiang province.
Taiwan reported 134 injuries linked to the storm.

articles.title: Russian strikes kill eight in Ukraine as Kyiv seeks faster weapons deliveries
bullets: Russian missile and drone attacks killed eight people and wounded dozens across Ukraine on Saturday.
Officials said glide bombs hit a crowded area in Sumy, killing five and injuring 30.
President Volodymyr Zelenskyy urged faster delivery of weapons and air-defense munitions.

articles.title: Venezuela quake toll rises above 4,300
bullets: Venezuela’s National Assembly president said the death toll from June 24 earthquakes rose to 4,333.
Officials reported 16,740 injured, about 17,000 homeless and 6,462 people rescued.
Authorities said initial housing distribution for affected residents would begin next week.

articles.title: UK police arrest man over killing of former minister Ann Widdecombe
bullets: British police arrested a 28-year-old man in South Yorkshire on suspicion of murdering Ann Widdecombe.
Police said they had no information suggesting terrorism or a political motive.
Widdecombe, 78, was a former Conservative minister and later a Reform UK figure.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top India-related stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: INS Mahendragiri commissioned into Indian Navy
bullets: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh commissioned INS Mahendragiri into the Eastern Fleet at Visakhapatnam.
The Project 17A stealth frigate has more than 75% indigenous content.
Officials said the warship can support anti-air, anti-surface and anti-submarine operations.

articles.title: Jaishankar to launch India’s UNSC campaign for 2028–29 term
bullets: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar is set to launch India’s campaign at UN headquarters in New York.
India is seeking a non-permanent United Nations Security Council seat for the 2028–29 term.
The campaign begins ahead of UN General Assembly elections scheduled for 2027.

articles.title: West Bengal forms panel to review draft Uniform Civil Code bill
bullets: The West Bengal government set up a high-level committee to examine a draft Uniform Civil Code bill.
The panel is headed by former Supreme Court judge Justice Ranjana Prakash Desai.
A state notification cited the wide ramifications and large scope of the proposed legislation.

articles.title: Cash and weapons seized from late Maharashtra don Baba Farzan’s home
bullets: Police raided Baba Farzan’s bungalow in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar about six months after his death.
Officials said they found more than ₹5.26 crore in unaccounted cash and multiple weapons.
Investigators also recovered jewelry, liquor bottles and financial documents.

articles.title: Two Indian tourists critical after Vietnam boat capsizes, killing 15
bullets: A speedboat carrying 32 Indian tourists and four Vietnamese crew members capsized near Phu Quoc.
Officials said 15 people died and two Indian tourists remained in critical condition.
The trip was organized for employees, distributors and retail partners of Lava International.

stocks.label: US Stocks
stocks.subtitle: Top US market movers and stock catalysts

articles.title: Meta shares surge on AI infrastructure cost optimism
bullets: Meta stock rose as investors focused on a Reuters-reviewed internal memo about AI compute expansion plans.
BofA said Meta’s implied compute-capacity cost could be far below earlier Wall Street assumptions.
The report also pointed to Meta’s planned custom AI chip roadmap.

articles.title: Micron jumps after expanding US investment plans
bullets: Micron shares settled 4.5% higher in Thursday trading.
The company said it would increase planned US investment to more than $250 billion through 2035.
It also committed up to $3 billion to strengthen the domestic semiconductor supply chain.

articles.title: PepsiCo falls despite revenue beat
bullets: PepsiCo reported second-quarter revenue above expectations.
Adjusted earnings came in slightly below analyst estimates.
Shares ended 3.3% lower after the report.

articles.title: AstraZeneca and Ionis slide after Wainua trial setback
bullets: US-listed AstraZeneca shares fell 5.9% after a late-stage Wainua trial failed in a rare heart-disease indication.
Partner Ionis Pharmaceuticals tumbled nearly 24%.
The drug setback weighed on biotech and pharma sentiment in the session.

articles.title: T-Mobile among Street’s highlighted upgrade calls
bullets: BofA Securities upgraded T-Mobile US to Buy with a $220 price target.
The firm said T-Mobile remained relatively insulated from satellite-broadband disruption risks.
Investing.com also highlighted analyst upgrade calls on Occidental, Five Below and Toll Brothers.

articles.title: Iraq PM to visit Washington with oil and gas deals expected
bullets: Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi will travel to Washington on Monday with an official delegation.
A spokesperson said planned agreements include memorandums of understanding in oil and gas.
Iraq is seeking US company involvement to increase production capacity and diversify export outlets.

articles.title: Japan eyes more alternative assets for world’s largest pension fund
bullets: Japan aims to raise alternative investments in the Government Pension Investment Fund, Nikkei reported.
Alternatives accounted for 1.7% of GPIF assets in March, below the 5% cap.
The move is intended to broaden pension asset management and reduce overall investment risk.

articles.title: Egypt current-account deficit more than doubles to $5.1 billion
bullets: Egypt’s January–March current-account deficit widened to $5.1 billion from $2.3 billion a year earlier.
The central bank cited a larger merchandise trade deficit as the main driver.
Remittances, tourism revenue and Suez Canal receipts partly offset the gap.

articles.title: Volkswagen unveils ‘Future Plan’ as competition pressure builds
bullets: Morgan Stanley said Volkswagen’s new plan marks a needed strategic shift.
The automaker is trying to restore competitiveness against Chinese rivals.
Analysts warned execution risks remain significant.

articles.title: AI investment boom seen adding to US inflation
bullets: CIBC Capital Markets estimated AI-related investment is adding roughly 0.4 percentage points to US inflation in 2026.
The report cited demand for data centers, computing equipment, electricity, chips and construction inputs.
It said productivity gains from AI are likely to arrive later than the current cost pressures.

articles.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade-secret theft
bullets: Apple filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI, io Products and former Apple employees of trade-secret misappropriation.
The complaint alleges confidential hardware information was used to advance OpenAI’s consumer-device efforts.
OpenAI responded that it has no interest in other companies’ trade secrets.

articles.title: Meta AI infrastructure plan boosts confidence in capex efficiency
bullets: A Reuters-reviewed memo said Meta is planning major AI compute expansion through 2026 and 2027.
BofA analysts said implied cost per gigawatt may be significantly lower than prior estimates.
Meta is also preparing a custom chip, code-named Iris, for manufacturing in the fall.

articles.title: Zhipu founder backs open-source approach for frontier AI
bullets: Zhipu founder Tang Jie said advanced AI should remain broadly accessible rather than limited to select developers.
The Chinese startup released its GLM-5.2 model under an open-source license.
Tang said the company will prioritize long-horizon reasoning, agents and self-training models.

articles.title: BofA names South Korea and UAE as leading AI contenders beyond US and China
bullets: Bank of America said the US and China remain far ahead in the AI race.
The report identified South Korea as the strongest overall contender outside those two leaders.
The UAE, Canada, Germany, Israel, the Netherlands, Singapore, Switzerland and the UK were also highlighted.

articles.title: AI may improve market efficiency while raising tail-risk concerns
bullets: A Bernstein note said AI is helping analysts process filings, earnings reports and datasets faster.
The same report warned similar AI models could synchronize trading signals and crowd positions.
Analysts said AI could reduce average inefficiencies but worsen dislocations during market stress.
